full_text:
t-CyCIF protocol

Day 0

Preparations

Make sure that the tissue section being stained is not older than 6 months and has been stored at +40C.

Set the temperature of the oven to 560C, and double check the temperature using a thermometer.

Check the levels of xylene and alcohol in the dewaxing and rehydration series in the fume hood, if the levels are too low add more xylene or respective alcohol. Prepare fresh xylene and alcohol series after using them for 5 times.

Make sure that all the buffers and antibodies are available: antigen retrieval (100mM citric acid pH 6.0), odyssey blocking buffer, 30% H202, & 480mM NaOH, and 1x PBS.

Day 1 (approx. 6.5 h)

Dewaxing and antigen retrieval (approx. 2h 10 min)

Baking: place the slides vertically into a grey plastic slide holder and place it inside oven set to 560c for 30 min

Deparaffinization: take out the slide holder from the oven and place it in Xylene 5 min x 3. Make sure that the slide is immersed until the white labelling slide.

Rehydration: place the slide holder in 100% ethanol, 90% ethanol, 70% ethanol, 50% ethanol, 30% ethanol 1x 3 min each, and then in two successive 1x PBS solutions, 1 min each. Make sure that the slide is immersed until the white labelling slide.

Antigen retrieval: Just before use, prepare 10mM citric acid pH 6.0 from 100mM stock🡪 25mL of stock solution + 225mL of MQ.

Transfer the slides from grey plastic holder to white plastic holders placed horizontally, transfer the white plastic holder into glass dishes and fill the dish with 250 mL of 10mM citric acid pH 6.0 above prepared.

Place the glass dish with the slides immersed in 10mM citric acid pH 6.0 in a microwave at low power (600W) and boil 3x as follows: First set the time to 4 min for boiling, at the end leave the slides inside for 5 min. Second and third time set the time to 2 min for boiling, and leave the slides inside for 5 min between two successive boiling. Cool the slides to room temperature (approx. 20 min). Wash the slides 3 times with 1XPBS ~ 3 min each.

2. Pre-staining (approx. 2h)

Prepare a moist incubation chamber to minimize the evaporation: Wet regular tissue paper under the tap water, fold it so that it fits inside the grey slide box, two papers per box (one on each side). After cooling and washing from step 1.4., take out the slides one at a time, dry the surroundings of the tissue section using soft fiber free tissue paper, add 200-300uL odyssey blocking buffer per tissue section, and incubate for 30 min at room temperature. Do not spread the blocking buffer with a pipette tip as you might scratch the tissue while doing so. In addition, thorough drying of the surroundings of the tissue creates a hydrophobic surface so that the solution does not spread outside the tissue.

Tap off the blocking buffer from step 2.1. Pre-stain the slides by incubation with secondary antibodies and Hoechst (200-300uL per section, listed below) for 60 min at room temperature a moist chamber (from 2.1.).

Prepare cocktail of secondary antibodies and Hoechst in odyssey blocking buffer:

488 goat anti-rabbit 1:1000

555 donkey anti-goat 1:1000

647 goat anti-mouse 1: 1000

HOECHST 1:5000

Transfer the slides into a vertical grey plastic holder placed in a white plastic container with 1xPBS. Wash three times with 1xPBS ~ 3 min each.

3. Bleaching/Fluorophore inactivation (approx. 2h)

Prepare 50 mL bleaching solution in a falcon tube as follows:

- 40 ml 1xPBS

- 7.5ml 30% H2O2

- 2.5ml 480 mM NaOH

Pour the bleaching solution onto white slide box lid placed on top of a LED light.

Using forceps transfer the slides into a bleaching solution; make sure that the slides are completely immersed in the solution. Place another LED light on top of the slide box (step 3.2). Connect bottom and top LED lights to power.

Photo bleach the sides for 90 minutes at room temperature, during which change the buffer solution 2 times after every 30 min.

Wash in PBS x 4 ~ 3 min each.

NOTE: Only for cycle 0 and cycle 1 change the bleaching solution 2x after every 30 min. Rest of the cycles only 1x after 45 min.

4. Staining with secondaries and Hoechst (approx. 15 min)

4.1. During washing step from 3.4. Prepare cocktail of secondary antibodies and Hoechst in blocking buffer

488 goat anti-rabbit 1:2000

555 donkey anti-goat 1:2000

647 goat anti-mouse 1: 2000

HOECHST 1:10000

NOTE: dilutions are different from step 2.2.

4.2. Incubate with the secondary antibodies (1:2000) and Hoechst (1:10000) at 4°C overnight in a moist chamber (from 2.1.)

Day 2

Wash the slides in PBS x3 ~ 3 min each

Dry the surroundings of the tissue section using soft fiber free tissue paper. Pipette 100 uL per section 70% glycerol. Carefully lay the coverslip on top of the slide, avoid air bubbles.

Scan the slides

Note: Remove excess glycerol from the surrounding of the slides. Slide holder will become dirty if the slide edges are moist and contain excess glycerol.

De-coverslipping

Place the slides vertically in a grey slide holder and leave the slides approx. 5 min in PBS. Carefully lift the slides and place them in another grey slide holder in a white plastic container with 1xPBS.

Wash PBS x3 ~ 3 min each.

Staining with primary antibodies

Stain with unconjugated primary antibodies overnight at +4°C in Odyssey solution. Wash in PBS x3

~ 3 min each.

Day 3

Staining with secondary antibodies

Incubate secondary antibodies 1:2000 and Hoechst 1:10000 in Odyssey buffer for 1 hour at room temperature

Wash PBS x3 (3 min each)

Dry the surroundings of the tissue section using soft fiber free tissue paper. Pipette 100 uL per section 70% glycerol. Carefully lay the coverslip on top of the slide, avoid air bubbles.

Scan the slides

8. Cyclic phase – repeat steps as before

- Decoverslipping

- Photo bleaching 1.5 h

- Wash x4 and ~3 mins for each wash

- Stain with preconjugated antibodies and Hoechst 1:10000 in Odyssey buffer overnight at +4°C.

- Wash PBS x3

- Mount and scan

Repeat step 8 for the next round
